summary: Matched Link Arrow's itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim by tightening local variable choices, stack padding, accessor macros, and related inline helper names in itlinkarrow.c. The PR also renamed the 0x802A850C Link Arrow function from generic it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C across symbols, header, and Link/Kirby callsites.
what_changed: Renamed symbol it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C in config/GALE01/symbols.txt while preserving address .text:0x802A850C and size 0x478.
Updated the itlinkarrow.h declaration from it_802A850C to itLinkArrow_802A850C.
Updated Link and Kirby special-N arrow callsites to call itLinkArrow_802A850C instead of the generic address-style name.
Renamed local inline helpers it_802A850C_inline and it_802A850C_inline_2 to itLinkArrow_802A850C_inline and itLinkArrow_802A850C_inline_2.
Adjusted local variable naming inside 0x802A850C: var_r30 became jobj and sp60 became flags.
Changed itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim helper code to initialize Item* and HSD_JObj* at declaration, using GET_ITEM(gobj) and GET_JOBJ(gobj).
Changed itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim from reading gobj->user_data directly to using GET_ITEM(gobj).
Modified stack padding declarations in itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim, replacing PAD_STACK(16) with explicit u8 pad[4] and u8 _pad[4] layout.
smart_moves: The PR did not only change the target animation function; it also propagated the newly identified Link Arrow function name through symbols, headers, and all known fighter callsites, keeping declarations and references consistent.
Using GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ instead of direct field access likely helped produce the desired code shape while aligning with project accessor conventions.
Renaming sp60 to flags documents the final out-parameter of it_8026EA20 without changing behavior, improving readability in a matched function.
The local padding change in itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim shows attention to exact stack layout, a common requirement for matching small animation callbacks.
Inline helper renames kept the 0x802A850C-related helper names tied to the now-named parent function, preserving grepability.
decomp_lessons: For item animation callbacks, direct gobj->user_data or gobj->hsd_obj access may not match as well as project macros such as GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, or HSD_GObjGetHSDObj; test accessor choices when the logic is equivalent.
Stack padding can be decisive for matching: replacing a generic PAD_STACK with explicit u8 padding variables may be necessary to reproduce the compiler's frame layout.
When a previously anonymous item function becomes understood, update the symbol map, header prototype, implementation, and every callsite in the same PR to avoid stale it_ address names.
Inline helper naming can be used as a decomp organization tactic: keep helper names scoped to the parent function when their identity is still based on the parent address.
Small local variable renames such as jobj, item, flags, and pad can clarify inferred semantics without disturbing generated code when done carefully.
naming_conventions: Named Link Arrow item functions use the prefix itLinkArrow_ for address-based global functions, as in itLinkArrow_802A850C.
Existing Link Arrow logic callbacks use mixed-case names such as itLinkArrow_Logic98_Destroyed and itLinkArrow_Logic98_PickedUp.
The target animation function in this slice is spelled itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim, with lowercase 'arrow' after Link in the existing function name.
Function-local inline helpers for a named function may append _inline and _inline_2 to the parent function name.
Generic temporary names like var_r30 and sp60 were replaced by semantic or conventional locals such as jobj and flags.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use GET_ITEM(gobj) rather than gobj->user_data when the macro form yields the expected code for item callbacks.
Use GET_JOBJ(gobj) instead of directly reading gobj->hsd_obj in helper code when matching requires the macro/access pattern.
Tune stack layout with explicit byte padding variables when PAD_STACK changes the frame shape unfavorably.
Declare and initialize locals in one statement where it matches the compiler output, as done for Item* item and HSD_JObj* jobj in itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline.
Preserve inline helper structure around HSD_JObjLoadJoint and quaternion setup while renaming helpers, indicating the match did not require algorithmic changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim or resolve the UnkMotion1 name.
Search for other itlinkarrow.c functions still using generic it_802A names near 0x802A8330 through 0x802A8A7C.
Look for matching examples where GET_ITEM or GET_JOBJ replaced direct gobj field access.
Check whether other item animation callbacks require explicit u8 padding instead of PAD_STACK.
